Required change: Replace blue/grey and low-contrast colour tokens; verify normal text >=4.5:1 and UI component boundaries >=3:1.
Test method: Automated contrast scan plus manual sample in light/dark and high-contrast mode.
Success criteria: All text and controls meet WCAG AA contrast; no information is conveyed by colour alone.

Required change: Ensure every control, menu and form is reachable and visible by keyboard; restore focus outline.
Success criteria: No keyboard trap; focus order logical; focus indicator visible and not obscured.

Required change: Add skip-to-main, semantic header/nav/main/footer and accessible page title.
Test method: Screen reader rotor/landmark check and keyboard tab order.
Success criteria: Users can bypass repeated content and identify page regions.

Required change: Associate labels/help text/errors programmatically with inputs; provide clear recovery guidance.
Test method: Automated labels scan plus NVDA/VoiceOver form completion.
Success criteria: Every input has a visible and programmatic label; errors are announced and actionable.

Required change: Simplify support ticket form, provide examples, autocomplete and confirmation summary.
Test method: Usability test with disabled employee participants; compare completion time and error rate.
Success criteria: >=90% task completion in moderated accessibility test; critical errors below agreed threshold.

Required change: Prioritise HR forms; improve headings, labels, confirmation and reversible submission where appropriate.
Test method: Manual screen reader and cognitive walkthrough.
Success criteria: Critical HR tasks can be completed independently with clear review/undo where needed.

Required change: Restructure mandatory training table, filters and course cards with semantic headings and labels.
Test method: Screen reader navigation plus browser zoom/reflow testing.
Success criteria: Users can find assigned training and status without relying on visual layout or colour.

Required change: Reduce competing notices, animations, dense link lists and unclear calls to action; create content hierarchy.
Test method: Heuristic review and user testing with neurodivergent/low-vision employees.
Success criteria: Primary task path is visually prominent; pages pass agreed readability and task success checks.

Required change: Replace 'click here', 'more details' and ambiguous links with descriptive text.
Test method: Automated link text report plus screen reader links list.
Success criteria: Links make sense out of context and duplicate labels do not point to different destinations.

Required change: Make layouts reflow to 320px width and support 200% text resize without loss of content/function.
Test method: Browser zoom, mobile width and spacing override test.
Success criteria: No horizontal scrolling for core content; controls remain usable at 200% zoom.

Required change: Remove incorrect ARIA, add accessible names, roles and live-region status only where needed.
Test method: Automated accessibility tree checks and manual screen reader validation.
Success criteria: Controls expose correct role, name, value and status updates.

Required change: Make search keyboard accessible, labelled, and operable with visible submit/status feedback.
Test method: Keyboard + screen reader test; no pointer-only shortcuts.
Success criteria: Search can be initiated and results understood without a mouse.

Required change: Publish internal accessibility statement, known issues list, contact route and SLA for fixes.
Success criteria: Statement is current, owner-named, and linked from all affected portals.

Required change: Create reusable accessible components, contribution rules, code review checklist and definition of done.
Test method: Sample code review and sprint audit.
Success criteria: New work cannot pass done criteria without automated and manual a11y evidence.

Required change: Add accessibility tags in service desk and analytics events for failed journeys, focus loss, form errors and feedback.
Test method: Data quality test in dashboard and ticket taxonomy review.
Success criteria: Monthly dashboard shows accessibility defects, SLA, task success and employee feedback.

Required change: Train content owners, designers, developers, QA and service desk on role-specific accessibility responsibilities.
Test method: Training completion records and practical assessment.
Success criteria: >=95% completion for named owners; sampled content passes checklist.
